description:
Goal: publish Compiler2-owned ABI facts from a closed MaterializedProgram without reopening semantic discovery. Signal: AbiReadyProgram(root) contains all ABI lane and return-delivery facts a backend needs for the closed executable frontier.

Change:
- Add Compiler2 artifact data types such as AbiValueRepr, ReturnAbi, AbiReadyProgram, AbiReadyExecutable, and AbiReadyCallEdge.
- Add a fact/job path from MaterializedProgram(root) to AbiReady(root).
- Derive value, parameter, return, and call-edge ABI from existing Ty ids and ExecutableNeed.
- Preserve extern_marshals and EffectSummary exactly from the materialized artifact.
- Treat unsupported or missing materialized facts as fatal artifact errors; do not ask semantic maps to fill gaps.

Why:
- Backends should consume ABI lanes and return contracts as closed facts, not recompute them from types and body shapes.

Checks:
- Quicksort proves partition/4 tuple-field return ABI is represented explicitly.
- Variadic extern fixtures prove frozen extern marshal classes flow into AbiReadyProgram.
- Tests assert no old planner/codegen telemetry is emitted while deriving ABI facts.
- Telemetry carries raw root ids, fact ids, and opaque AbiReadyProgram data only.
title: Compiler2 artifacts: derive AbiReadyProgram from MaterializedProgram
